methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
First Light Fusion is a Series C nuclear energy company that has raised over $100M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method here. Using the $100M total capital raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C progression yields an implied valuation of $170M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow nuclear energy frontier-tech subset; while 8 same-sector peers exist, none yielded three or more datapoints meeting the stage and valuation-tier filters required for statistical confidence.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $144M to $195M, with $170M as the median point derived from stage-progression alone. The single-method result reflects the inherent constraints of valuing frontier-stage nuclear technology where public market comparables remain thin and private peer financials are sparse or non-disclosed. Key uncertainties include technology-risk premia specific to First Light's projectile fusion approach, the pace of regulatory pathway clarification, and capital intensity requirements through commercialization.
